article_meta.title: President Donald Trump dismissed sex offender Jeffrey Epstein as “disgusting” and decried his procurer, Ghislaine Maxwel

reasoning: One genuine author-prose bias was correctly identified, but the second item includes quoted text (attribution violation). The model also used non-standard bias type labels and missed other clear instances in the author’s prose.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: Item 2 embeds quoted material (“was one of the very first people to call”) within the flagged text, violating the rule to avoid quoting sources’ language.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Both flagged passages point to real slant in the author’s prose; no clear false positives.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Missed additional author-level bias such as the un-attributed label “procurer” for Maxwell and the sensational “RELATED: RELEASE THE FILES!” link framing.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Used non-listed labels (“Loaded Language”, “Selection of Sources/Framing”) instead of valid types like Word Choice, Slant, or Sensationalism/Emotionalism.
